stimulus: Recent radiocarbon dating of the Saharan rock art at Tassili n'Ajjer has prompted some archaeologists to claim that the now arid plateau once supported herding societies. This claim ______blank that certain bovine species, such as Bos primigenius, grazed there without interruption from the early Holocene onward cannot stand, researchers Aïcha Bouchareb and Élise Renaud caution, unless competing reconstructions can first be eliminated.
stem: Which choice completes the text so that it conforms to the conventions of Standard English?
rationale:
Choice D is the best answer. The convention being tested is the use of verb forms within a sentence. The nonfinite present participle "asserting" is correctly used to form a restrictive participial phrase ("asserting…onward") within the main clause ("This claim…cannot stand"). This participial phrase functions as part of the sentence's subject, providing essential identifying information about what the claim states—namely, that certain bovine species grazed there without interruption from the early Holocene onward.

Choice A is incorrect because it results in an ungrammatical sentence. The finite verb "asserts" can't be used in this way within the subject of the sentence. Choice B is incorrect because it results in an ungrammatical sentence. The finite verb "asserted" can't be used in this way within the subject of the sentence. Choice C is incorrect because it results in an ungrammatical sentence. The finite verb "has asserted" can't be used in this way within the subject of the sentence.
